titleStmt.title: SII 28.54: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 54. (A.R. No. 53 of 1913.) TIRUCHCHEṄGĀṬṬĀṄGUḌI, NANNILAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: Uttarāpatīśvara temple, central shrine, south wall of the maṇḍapa in front.

Demaged. In late characters. States that some Cheṭṭiyār (śrēshṭis) belonging to the group Vaiśya-nagarattār deposited into the temple-treasury a throne for Uttarapati-nāya[kar]. It seems that in recognition of this, a house-site was granted to them as maṭam. An extent of 2 vēli also seems to have been gifted to them as maṭam probably the house-site was for setting up the maṭam. It is possible that the maṭam was already in existence under their control and the grant of 2 vēli of cultivable land was for meeting the running expenses of their maṭam. It is also stated that 200 paṇam for gold jewels of 15 kaḻañju and 50 paṇam for a veḷḷi-orrai (?) (silver ornament) had also been granted, probably by the cheṭṭiyārs. It appears that actually it was against this donation the house-site and the 2 Vēli of land had been gifted to the cheṭṭiyārs. the land concerned made as sarvvamānya (ie) it was exempt from all upādis like old tax (paḻavari) and new tax (puduvari), and śoṟu-māṭṭu. This record equates 15 kaḻañju to 200 paṇam.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śubhamastu . vicuvāvacu varuṣam Āvaṇi māsam 10 2 tirucceṅkāṭṭaṅkuṭi nayiṉār Uttirāpatiṉāyakar koyil
paṇṭārattil Uttarāpati vaiśya nakarattār veṭṭaṉ tai vetakiri ceṭṭiyārum puṟappavāṇiya tippu ceṭṭiyārum
kāmāṇṭai ceṭṭiyār Uḷḷiyṭṭārkku kuṭutta kaiyittaṭi tāṅkaḷ nayiṉār Uttarāpatiṉāya
The stone containing the begining of this and following five lines seems to have been lost.paḷḷi caṅkāsanam paṇṇi cāttukaiyil taṅkaḷukku maṭamāka kuṭutta cila ṟakka naṭa ta ṉa
malai tiruvītiyil mel ciṟakil ceṭṭiyār Uttirāpatiṉāyakar maṇaikku teṟkku
var pūcaikku taṉta rūpāyThis symbol represents nilam2li Iṉta nilam Iruvelikkum tiruvāparaṇam Ā ṇi taṉta mā tiṉa
kku poṉ kaḻañcu15kku mātamThis symol stands for paṇam or kaḻañju. 2 100 veḷḷi Oṟṟaikku mātam 5 10 Āka mātam 2 100 5 10 Ippaṇam Irunūṟṟu Aṉpatukkum
maṉaiyum ṉilam Iruveliyum caṉtirātittavaraiyum sarvva māṉṉiya Aṉupavittu
koḷḷavum rūpāy Iruvelikku EllaiThe adjacent stone to contain the continuation of previous five lines and there is a sixth line reading ta[lai] ••• [lu]kkuvelimaṭaiyār kuvaḷai
naṭuvil Erikaraikku kiḻakku peruvākkālu vaṭa kaṭṭaḷai vākkālukku vaṭakku Īñcam
rūpāyThis symbol represents nilam (1/2) Āka rūpāy Iruveliyum maṭamum A vācalil puṟakkum paḻavari putuvari Eṟ
peṟpaṭṭa cakala Upātiyum coṟumāṭṭum cantirātittavaraiyum caṟuvamāṉiyam Āka Aṉupavittu koḷḷavum
Digital edition of SII 28.54 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
